q1.prompt: A 32-year-old female greenhouse worker presents with an ulcerative nodule on her right distal forearm that has persisted for over a month. She recalls receiving multiple scratches from thorn-covered plants in that area. If the biopsy material from this lesion were cultured in the laboratory at 37°C, which of the following morphologies would be expected?

q2.prompt: A 32-year-old previously healthy female in Thailand presents with fever, pleuritic chest pain, and a productive cough. A chest CT scan reveals a large solitary pulmonary nodule. A fine-needle aspirate of the lesion demonstrates encapsulated budding yeasts. The patient reports that she lives near a large grove of eucalyptus trees. Which of the following organisms is the most likely causative agent of her infection?

q3.prompt: A 45-year-old patient with poorly controlled Type 1 Diabetes presents with paranasal swelling, bloody nasal exudate, and a black necrotic eschar on the palate. A tissue biopsy reveals broad, irregular, non-septate hyphae branching at wide angles. Which of the following triazole antifungal agents is identified in the sources as an appropriate alternative for treating this specific serious infection?

q4.prompt: A 40-year-old patient from Southeast Asia with AIDS presents with fever and weight loss. A thermally dimorphic fungus is isolated from the patient. When grown as a mold at room temperature (25°C), the colony is characterized by the production of a soluble red pigment. Which of the following is the most likely causative agent?

q5.prompt: A patient is prescribed terbinafine for the treatment of onychomycosis. Which of the following describes the primary mechanism by which this medication exerts its antifungal activity?

options.label: Binding to tubulin to disrupt the mitotic spindle
Formation of membrane pores by binding to ergosterol
Inhibition of 1,3-β-D-glucan synthase in the cell wall
Inhibition of the fungal enzyme squalene epoxidase

q6.prompt: A 24-year-old male with AIDS and a CD4+ count of 60/µL presents with a non-productive cough, fever, and night sweats. A chest X-ray shows diffuse ground-glass opacities. Which of the following is the first-line therapeutic and primary prophylactic agent for the most likely cause of this pneumonia?

q7.prompt: A patient with a history of tuberculosis has a follow-up chest CT scan that demonstrates a "fungus ball" (aspergilloma) within a pre-existing pulmonary cavity. Which of the following serum biomarkers is specifically noted in the sources for its utility in detecting invasive infections with this septate mold?

q8.prompt: A 35-year-old biologist presents with a 10-day history of high fever, headache, and a nonproductive cough. He recently spent several days collecting samples inside a hollow tree containing extensive bat nests and guano. A chest radiograph demonstrates bilateral pulmonary infiltrates. Histopathological examination of a lung biopsy reveals necrotizing granulomatous inflammation. Which of the following microscopic features is most characteristic of the causative pathogen when observed in human tissue?

options.label: Round budding yeasts with a thick capsule

q9.prompt: Echinocandin medications, such as caspofungin and micafungin, are often used for invasive candidiasis. Which of the following describes the specific mode of action of this class of antifungals?

options.label: Binding to ergosterol to create pores in the cell membrane
Conversion to 5-fluorouracil to block DNA biosynthesis
Inhibition of 1,3-β-D-glucan synthesis in the cell wall
Inhibition of squalene epoxidase in the sterol pathway
Interference with microtubule function to inhibit mitosis

q10.prompt: A skin scraping is collected from a patient with hypopigmented macules on the chest and treated with 10% potassium hydroxide (KOH). Microscopic examination reveals short, curved hyphae and clusters of budding yeast, described as a "spaghetti and meatballs" appearance. What is the most likely causative organism?
